data.goal: Compare the standard API input price per 1 million tokens (base/standard tier) for three frontier LLMs — Google Gemini 2.5 Pro, Anthropic Claude Opus 4.x, and OpenAI's current GPT-5 flagship — and determine which has the cheapest input price and the figure.

   https://pricepertoken.com/pricing-page/model/google-gemini-2.5-pro
   Gemini 2.5 Pro was released on June 17, 2025. Pricing starts at $1.00 per million input tokens and $10.00 per million output tokens. The model supports a context window of up to 1.0M tokens. API access is available through Google.

input.query: OpenAI GPT-5 API price per million tokens 2025

data.subquestion: What is the current API input price per 1M tokens for GPT-5?
data.rationale: Need to find the current pricing for OpenAI GPT-5 to compare.

data.summary:
Search results for 'OpenAI GPT-5 API price per million tokens 2025' (via duckduckgo):
1. API Pricing - OpenAI
   https://openai.com/api/pricing/
   Explore OpenAI API pricing for GPT-5.5, multimodal models, and tools. Compare token costs, realtime, image, and video pricing, plus service tiers.······
2. Pricing | OpenAI API
   https://developers.openai.com/api/docs/pricing
   For gpt-4o-mini and gpt-4.1-mini with the non-preview web search tool, search content tokens are billed as a fixed block of 8,000 input tokens per call. File search tool call pricing applies to the Responses API only. Container pricing includes Hosted Shell and Code Interpreter.
3. LLM API Pricing Comparison (2025): OpenAI, Gemini, Claude
   https://intuitionlabs.ai/articles/llm-api-pricing-comparison-2025
   A complete LLM API pricing comparison for 2025. Analyze token-based costs for OpenAI (GPT-5), Google Gemini, Anthropic Claude, Grok, and DeepSeek models.
4. GPT 5 API Pricing 2026 - Costs, Performance & Providers
   https://pricepertoken.com/pricing-page/model/openai-gpt-5
   GPT 5 was released on August 7, 2025. Pricing starts at $0.625 per million input tokens and $5.00 per million output tokens. The model supports a context window of up to 400K tokens. API access is available through OpenAI.
5. OpenAI API Pricing 2026: GPT-5.5, GPT-5.4, o3 & All Models
   https://www.aipricing.guru/openai-pricing/
   OpenAI API pricing for June 2026. Compare GPT-5.5, GPT-5.5 Pro, GPT-5.4, GPT-4.1, o3, and GPT-4o token costs with input, output, and cached rates.
6. OpenAI API Pricing 2026: Complete Guide to GPT-4o, GPT-5.5 & Realtime ...
   https://the-rogue-marketing.github.io/openai-api-pricing-comparison-october-2025/
   Stop overpaying for OpenAI APIs. Check our complete, updated May 2026 breakdown of GPT-4o, GPT-5.5, Realtime audio, and DALL-E 4 pricing.
7. LLM API Pricing Comparison 2025: GPT-5, Claude, Gemini, DeepSeek
   https://ailog.page/the-price-war-that-made-ai-cheap-a-complete-llm-api-pricing-guide/
   GPT-4's initial pricing set the standard at $30/$60 per million input/output tokens. Today, GPT-5 delivers better performance at $1.25/$10 — a 95% price reduction for superior capability.
8. OpenAI GPT-5.5 API Pricing Explained: Real Production Cost at 10M+ Tokens
   https://markaicode.com/pricing/openai-api-gpt-55-pricing-cost-per-million-tokens/
   OpenAI GPT-5.5 API pricing per million tokens explained: input/output costs, hidden fees, and optimization tactics for developers estimating cloud AI budgets.

data.reason: previous web_fetch came back 'irrelevant' — re-routing to web_fetch(https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/about-claude/pricing).

data.summary:
Read https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/about-claude/pricing:
According to the official Anthropic pricing page, Claude Opus 4.x models (versions 4.5, 4.6, 4.7, and 4.8) all have a standard base input price of $5 per million tokens (MTok). The deprecated Claude Opus 4.1 had a higher rate of $15/MTok, while the retired Claude Opus 4 also charged $15/MTok.
Key facts:
  - Claude Opus 4.8: $5 / MTok base input price
  - Claude Opus 4.7: $5 / MTok base input price
  - Claude Opus 4.6: $5 / MTok base input price
  - Claude Opus 4.5: $5 / MTok base input price
  - Claude Opus 4.1 (deprecated): $15 / MTok base input price
  - MTok = Million tokens; Base Input Tokens column shows standard input pricing

extract.summary: According to the official Anthropic pricing page, Claude Opus 4.x models (versions 4.5, 4.6, 4.7, and 4.8) all have a standard base input price of $5 per million tokens (MTok). The deprecated Claude Opus 4.1 had a higher rate of $15/MTok, while the retired Claude Opus 4 also charged $15/MTok.
key_facts: Claude Opus 4.8: $5 / MTok base input price
Claude Opus 4.7: $5 / MTok base input price
Claude Opus 4.6: $5 / MTok base input price
Claude Opus 4.5: $5 / MTok base input price
Claude Opus 4.1 (deprecated): $15 / MTok base input price
MTok = Million tokens; Base Input Tokens column shows standard input pricing

According to the official pricing stated in this billing inquiry, Gemini 2.5 Pro has an input price of $1.25 per 1M tokens for prompts ≤ 200k tokens. The user references this official rate while reporting a billing discrepancy where actual charges were significantly higher than expected based on these official prices.
Key facts:

  - Official output price: $10.00 per 1M tokens (for prompts ≤ 200k tokens)
  - Prices apply specifically to prompts with ≤ 200k tokens
  - User reported actual billing was approximately 30x higher than expected based on official pricing

data.summary:
Search results for 'OpenAI "gpt-5" API pricing "$1.25" OR "$2.50" OR "$5" per million input tokens official' (via duckduckgo):
1. OpenAI API Pricing | OpenAI
   https://openai.com/api/pricing/
   Explore OpenAI API pricing for GPT-5.5, multimodal models, and tools. Compare token costs, realtime, image, and video pricing, plus service tiers.
2. GPT-5 - API Pricing & Benchmarks | OpenRouter
   https://openrouter.ai/openai/gpt-5
   $1.25 per million input tokens, $10 per million output tokens. 400,000 token context window, maximum output of 128,000 tokens. Higher uptime with 2 providers. Includes independent benchmarks from Artificial Analysis.Input Price.
3. What Is GPT-5.5? OpenAI's New Frontier Model Explained
   https://apidog.com/blog/what-is-gpt-5-5/
   API pricing lands at $5 per million input tokens and $30 per million output tokens; double GPT-5.4’s rate, offset by tighter token usage.OpenAI has not deprecated 5.4, and it stays cheaper at $2.50 / $15 per million tokens.
4. GPT-5.5 Review 2026: Benchmarks, Reactions & Real Analysis
   https://www.buildfastwithai.com/blogs/gpt-5-5-review-benchmarks-2026
   How much does GPT-5.5 cost? API pricing is $5 per million input tokens and $30 per million output tokens -- a 2x increase over GPT-5.4. OpenAI claims 40% token efficiency gains in Codex workflows, which reduces the effective cost increase to around 20% for agentic use cases.
5. GPT-5 Nano: $0.05/$0.40 Pricing, 400K Context, Still... - TokenMix Blog
   https://tokenmix.ai/blog/gpt-5-nano-benchmarks-api-access-use-cases-2026
   OpenAI's GPT-5 Nano is the cheapest OpenAI chat model from the original GPT-5 generation — $0.05 per million input tokens, $0.40 per million output, 400K token context, optimized for summarization and classification.
6. GPT-5.4 Can Now Use Your Computer: OpenAI's Most Agentic Mo
   https://udit.co/blog/openai-gpt-5-4-computer-use-million-token-context
   API pricing for GPT-5.4 through the Responses API: Standard input (up to 272K tokens): $2.50 per million tokens.
7. GPT-5.5 Is Twice as Expensive as GPT-5.4. OpenRouter Revealed...
   https://www.ai-jarvis.eu/gpt-55-twice-expensive-gpt-54-openrouter-revealed-how-much-it-actually-costs
   OpenAI's pricing policy cannot be judged in isolation. Anthropic offers the Claude Opus 4.7 model for $5 per million input tokens and $25 per million output tokens. This means GPT-5.5 is equally expensive on input but 20% more expensive on output.
8. GPT-5 Mini: The Best AI Model for Price, Speed & Power
   https://arsturn.com/blog/gpt-5-mini-underrated-model-price-speed-deep-dive
   It’s set at $0.25 per million input tokens & $2.00 per million output tokens. To put that in perspective: The flagship GPT-5 costs $1.25 for input & $10.00 for output. That's 5x more expensive on the input & 5x on the output.

data.answer:
# API Input Price Comparison: Gemini 2.5 Pro vs. Claude Opus 4.x vs. GPT-5

## Pricing Summary (Standard/Base Tier, per 1M Input Tokens)

| Model | Provider | Input Price (per 1M tokens) | Output Price (per 1M tokens) |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Gemini 2.5 Pro** | Google | **$1.25** (≤200K ctx) / $2.50 (>200K ctx) | $10.00 |
| **Claude Opus 4.x** | Anthropic | **$5.00** (current versions: 4.5–4.8) | $25.00 |
| **GPT-5** | OpenAI | **$1.25** | $10.00 |

---

## Key Findings

### 🏆 Cheapest Input Price (Tie): **Google Gemini 2.5 Pro** & **OpenAI GPT-5** — both at **$1.25 / 1M input tokens**

- **Google Gemini 2.5 Pro**: $1.25/1M input tokens for prompts up to 200K tokens; rises to $2.50/1M for longer contexts. Output is $10.00/1M tokens [1][2][3].
- **OpenAI GPT-5**: $1.25/1M input tokens (standard tier), $10.00/1M output tokens. Released August 7, 2025; 400K context window [4][5].
- **Anthropic Claude Opus 4.x**: The most expensive of the three at **$5.00/1M input tokens** for current active versions (4.5, 4.6, 4.7, 4.8) and $25.00/1M output tokens. (Note: the original Claude Opus 4.0 and 4.1 were priced at $15/1M input but are now retired/deprecated.) [6]

---

## Notes & Caveats

1. **Claude Opus 4.x pricing has dropped significantly**: Early Opus 4 was $15/1M input tokens, but Anthropic cut prices substantially with versions 4.5 onward, now at $5/1M.
2. **Gemini 2.5 Pro has a tiered context price**: The $1.25 rate applies only to prompts ≤200K tokens. Longer prompts cost $2.50/1M input tokens [2][3].
3. **GPT-5 vs. GPT-5.x variants**: OpenAI's lineup has since expanded to GPT-5.4 ($2.50/1M) and GPT-5.5 ($5.00/1M), but the base **GPT-5** model remains at $1.25/1M input tokens [4][5].
4. One aggregator (pricepertoken.com) listed GPT-5 at $0.625/1M — this may reflect a cached/discounted rate or a data error; the majority of corroborating sources confirm **$1.25/1M** as the standard rate [4][5][7].

citations.note: Corroborates GPT-5 at $1.25/1M input tokens and $10/1M output tokens as of November 2025.

citations.title: Pricing – Claude API Docs (Anthropic Official)
citations.note: Official Anthropic pricing page: Claude Opus 4.5–4.8 at $5/1M input tokens, $25/1M output tokens. Retired Opus 4.0 and deprecated 4.1 were $15/1M input.

citations.title: GPT-5 Mini: The Best AI Model for Price, Speed & Power
citations.note: States the flagship GPT-5 costs $1.25/1M input tokens and $10/1M output tokens, with GPT-5 Mini at $0.25/1M input.

data.confidence: medium — High confidence on Claude Opus 4.x ($5/1M) from Anthropic's own docs. Medium-high confidence on Gemini 2.5 Pro ($1.25/1M) from multiple corroborating sources referencing Google's official pricing page. Medium confidence on GPT-5 ($1.25/1M) from third-party aggregators (OpenRouter, OpenRouter corroborated by two articles); OpenAI's own pricing page was fetched but listed GPT-5.5/5.4 as current flagships without a plain "gpt-5" line visible, suggesting the model may have been superseded in their UI.
data.open_gaps: 1. OpenAI's own pricing page no longer prominently lists a plain "GPT-5" model (showing GPT-5.4 at $2.50/1M and GPT-5.5 at $5/1M as the current flagships), so the designation of "current GPT-5 flagship" is ambiguous. If GPT-5.5 is now the flagship, it ties with Claude Opus 4.x at $5/1M input. 2. The pricepertoken.com figure of $0.625/1M for GPT-5 could not be definitively reconciled — it may represent a cached/discounted tier. 3. Google's Gemini API pricing page has been updated to list Gemini 3.x models, suggesting Gemini 2.5 Pro pricing may have changed or been delisted from the primary page.
